calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-01-2020 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-02-2020 Continued To Dec-16-20 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Petition For Order To Arbitration
Off Calendar Feb-24-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Heather Grace Hensley, Esq. of BERTRAND FOX ELLIOT OSMAN & WENZEL, T:415.353.0999 ext 127, for defendant/moving party;
Scott Love, Esq., T:415.216.9190, for plaintiff/opposing party.

RULING: The matter was passed to allow plaintiff's counsel to read the Court's tentative ruling. Plaintiff's counsel submitted to the Court's tentative ruling. Therefore, there being no objection, the tentative ruling is adopted on DEFENDANTS SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT, BRENT STEPHENS, JONATHAN OSLER's Petition For Order To Arbitration as follows:

Defendants San Francisco Unified School District, Brent Stephens, and Jonathan Olser's motion to compel arbitration and stay is granted. Plaintiff's broad arbitration agreement with USF applies to plaintiff's claims and the non-signatory movants can also compel arbitration based upon estoppel and agency.

[See word minutes for the Court's complete ruling.]

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-03-2019 Continued To Jun-17-20 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-22-2019 Continued To Dec-18-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Order To Arbitrate And Stay Action By Defendants Community Initiatives And Peter Williamson
Off Calendar Nov-01-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Scott Love, Esq. 415-216-9190 of Love Law for Plaintiff.
Michael Vartain, Esq. 415-391-1155 of Vartain Law Group for all Defendants.
Ethan Lowry, Esq. 415-353-0999 of Bertrand for SFUSD Defendants.

RULING - A R G U E D. Plaintiff contests the second motion only and argues. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT COMMUNITY INITIATIVES, PETER WILLIAMSON'S Petition For Order To Arbitrate And Stay Action By Defendants Community Initiatives And Peter Williamson.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Defendants Community Initiatives and Peter Williamson's motion to compel arbitration and stay is granted. The broad arbitration agreement with USF applies to plaintiff's claims. USF is clearly entitled to compel arbitration. The non-signatories can also compel arbitration based upon estoppel and agency. "[A] nonsignatory defendant may invoke an arbitration clause to compel a signatory plaintiff to arbitrate its claim when the causes of action against the nonsignatory are intimately founded in and intertwined with the underlying contract obligations. The doctrine applies where the claims are based on the same facts and are inherently inseparable from the arbitrable claims against signatory defendants." (Garcia v. Pexco, LLC (2017) 11 Cal.App.5th 782, 786 (internal quotations and citations omitted); see also Vasquez v. San Miguel Produce, Inc. (2019) 31 Cal.App.5th 810, 816 [a "co-employer relationship and identity of interest with regard to their mutual employees allows [non-signatory defendants] to compel arbitration of an employment dispute"]; Martinez v. Scott Specialty Gases, Inc. (2000) 83 Cal.App.4th 1236, 1245 [agent can compel arbitration].) Plaintiff's claims regarding his employment after 10/6/16 are inextricably intertwined with his pre-10/6/16 claims and plaintiff must arbitrate the entire action. Plaintiff admits that he resumed his prior position and plaintiff's alleged "new employers" after 10/6/16 (e.g. SFTR and SFUSD) were plaintiff's joint employer prior to that time. (Compl. 58; see also, e.g., id. 59-61 [alleging that between October and November 2016, "Plaintiff continued experiencing, and reporting issues of," labor code violations and denial of promised benefits, and that between December 2016 to May 2017, he "exercised a series of protected activities," including participating "as a witness in the investigations that followed these activities, including issues from the June 2014 through September 30, 2016 employment."].) The FAA requires arbitration of the Bane Act claim. (Saheli v. White Memorial Medical Center (2018) 21 Cal.App.5th 308, 323 ["[S]ection 2 of the FAA "preempts any state rule discriminating on its face against arbitration-for example, a 'law prohibit[ing] outright the arbitration of a particular type of claim.'"] [holding that trial court erred in denying petition to compel arbitration of Bane Act claims].)

In addition, the action as to the moving Defendant is ordered to arbitration and is styed pending arbitration.

An order is signed for both motions. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

Scott Specialty Gases, Inc. (2000) 83 Cal.App.4th 1236, 1245 [agent can compel arbitration].)
